description: Generated clients turn small schema changes into compile-time or runtime client breakage.
body: Apply this when OpenAPI, JSON Schema, protobuf, tRPC, GraphQL codegen, or SDK generation exists in the repo. Removing fields, changing requiredness, narrowing enums, changing response unions, or renaming operations can break generated clients beyond the server tests. This does not cover SDK packaging mechanics except as evidence of contract consumption. Execution impact: run or inspect schema generation, compare generated type or client diffs, and update examples or compatibility layers when the diff is intended. Validation signals include generated client compiles, docs/examples match the new schema, and diff review shows only intentional additions or versioned breaks. Risk boundary: never hide a breaking schema diff by editing generated output directly without changing the source contract. Use when Backend API work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
